title: Configure Uniface for LDAP
text: Configure Uniface for LDAP To configure Uniface to connect to with LDAP, you need to edit the Uniface application assignment file. In the assignment file, specify the LDAP connector version you want to use in the [DRIVER_SETTINGS ] section. For example: [DRIVER_SETTINGS] LDP = U3.0 Copy Optionally, specify options to modify the behavior of the LDAP connector using USYS$LDP_PARAMS . For example: For more information, see LDAP Connector Options . [DRIVER_SETTINGS] LDP = U3.0 USYS$LDP_PARAMS tls server validation=ignore_name, nulldefault=N Copy Define the logon path in the [PATHS ] section. For example: www.traders.com is the server acting as host, and o=accounting.com is the organization used as the LDAP search base. [PATHS] $LDP LDP:www.traders.com+389:o=accounting.com| | Copy Define entity settings in the [ENTITIES] section to determine at which level of the LDAP tree entries are stored. Entity settings in the assignment file have the following syntax: Entity.Model = Entity @ Tree.Model Copy For example: person.ldap = person@ou=People,o=name.com.* Copy If this information is not specified, the path search base is used, in which case all database operations default to this level in the LDAP tree. Searches start here and new entries are stored at this level. Due to the nature of LDAP, the LDAP connector tries to store updated entries at this level even when they have been retrieved from a different level. To circumvent this behavior, it is possible to use entity redirecting to store entries at the correct level. Consider an LDAP tree which is structured as follows: o=accounting.com ou=people ou=finance Copy The following settings ensure that the person entity is stored at the search base level, while the person2 entity is redirected to another node. [PATHS] $LDP = LDP:www.traders.com+389:o=accounting.com|eddie|eddie [ENTITIES] person.ldap=person@o=accounting.com person2.ldap=person@ou=people,o=accounting.com *.ldap = $LDP:*.* Copy Related Links LDAP The Lghtweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) is a protocol for accessing online directory services. It offers common access to a centralized facility for storing and maintaining user access privilege, email, and sign-on information which can be shared among Web servers, mail servers, news servers and Uniface applications.
